Title: LRM3 positively regulates stem lodging resistance by degradating MYB6 transcriptional repressor in soybean

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stem mechanical strength and lodging resistance; supports yield stability
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation and stem strength, thereby enhancing lodging resistance and increasing pod number and grain weight per plant.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant U-box E3 ubiquitin ligase with U-box domain and six ARMADILLO repeats
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tissues, predominantly in leaves; expression in the fourth internode increases from V3 to V7 during stem lignification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tissues, predominantly in leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; EMS mutant identification; complementation; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; Y2H; split-LUC; Co-IP; pull-down; in vitro ubiquitination; cell-free degradation assay; metabolite/lignin profiling; haplotype analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LRM3 was cloned as Glyma.18G055200, and loss of function caused weak stems, lodging susceptibility, reduced lignin, and reduced yield-related traits. 2. Complementation restored the wild-type phenotype, whereas CRISPR knockout phenocopied lrm3 and overexpression increased stem strength and lignin. 3. LRM3 physically interacts with MYB6 and promotes its ubiquitination and 26S proteasome-dependent degradation. 4. LRM3 Hap1 was enriched in cultivated soybean and associated with higher lignin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong candidate for breeding soybean lodging resistance and yield stability by selecting favorable Hap1 alleles or by overexpression/editing strategies that enhance LRM3 activity while preserving agronomic performance.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces pod number and grain weight per plant; no clear penalty for overexpression reported, instead yield-related traits improved.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field and greenhouse growth; stem lignification stages V3-V7; MG132 treatment in degradation assays
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, Genome Sequence Archive: CRA024512; resequencing-based bulk segregant analysis; haplotype analysis using 1591 soybean accessions from NCBI SRA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LRM3 is the soybean homolog of Arabidopsis PUB4. A homologous gene, Glyma.11G161500, when knocked out did not affect stem strength. Natural variation at codon 360 (Cys/Arg) affected ubiquitination activity and was associated with domestication-related selection.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stem mechanical strength and lodging resistance; supports yield stability
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of lignin biosynthesis; its overexpression causes lodging and reduced stem lignification, whereas knockout increases lignin in hairy roots.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tissues, highest in leaves; expression in the fourth internode decreases from V3 to V7 and is lower in highly lignified stems
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tissues, highest in leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; hairy-root knockout; Y2H; split-LUC; Co-IP; pull-down; protein abundance assay; EMSA; CUT&Tag-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB6 directly interacts with LRM3 and accumulates to higher protein levels in the lrm3 background. 2. MYB6 is ubiquitinated and degraded in an LRM3- and 26S proteasome-dependent manner. 3. MYB6 directly binds the promoters of PAL1 and PAL2 and represses their expression. 4. MYB6 overexpression reduces lignification and causes lodging, while myb6 knockout increases lignin and suppresses the lrm3 defect in hairy roots.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful negative target for editing or downregulation to enhance lignin deposition and lodging resistance, potentially in combination with favorable LRM3 alleles.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes lodging phenotype and reduced lignification; broader effects on phenylpropanoid regulation beyond PAL genes may occur but were not fully characterized.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MG132 treatment in degradation assays; transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; soybean hairy-root transformation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: CUT&Tag-qPCR; RNA-seq context from lrm3-dependent transcriptome changes; accession CRA024512 for study RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Soybean homolog of poplar LTF1. Acts downstream of LRM3 as a transcriptional repressor in the lignin pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stem mechanical strength and lodging resistance; supports yield stability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin biosynthesis and stem strength; PAL1 mutation reduces PAL activity, lignin content, and lodging resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in lrm3 and lrm3CR1 stems; upregulated in LRM3 overexpression lines; repressed by MYB6 in W82 hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry/committed step of phenylpropanoid pathway supplying lignin monomers
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as initial committed/key control step in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant analysis; PAL activity assay; lignin quantification; lignin monomer profiling; EMSA; CUT&Tag-qPCR; RT-qPCR; transient promoter-LUC assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL1 contains an ACCTAC MYB-binding motif in its promoter and is directly bound and repressed by MYB6. 2. PAL1 transcript is reduced in lrm3 and elevated in LRM3 overexpression lines. 3. EMS-induced pal1 mutant shows reduced PAL activity, lower lignin, weaker stems, lodging, and reduced yield-related traits. 4. PAL1 inhibition is causally linked to the lrm3 lodging phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Positive engineering target for improving stem strength and lodging resistance; should be combined with upstream regulatory optimization such as enhancing LRM3 or reducing MYB6 repression.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces lignin, stem strength, pod number, and grain weight per plant.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stem analysis at V7 and R1 stages; field and greenhouse assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PAL1 native promoter; 35S promoter used for MYB6 repression assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem, especially lignified stem tissues/vascular tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: pal1 mutant carried a T to A transition in exon 2 causing a Thr-to-Ser substitution; mutant showed decreased G- and S-lignin but unchanged H-lignin.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stem mechanical strength and lodging resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin biosynthesis as a MYB6-repressed PAL gene downstream of LRM3.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in lrm3 and lrm3CR1 stems; upregulated in LRM3 overexpression lines; repressed by MYB6 in W82 hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry/committed step of phenylpropanoid pathway supplying lignin monomers
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, contributing to phenylpropanoid precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as part of the initial committed step module
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMSA; CUT&Tag-qPCR; RT-qPCR; transient promoter-LUC assay; expression analysis in lrm3 and LRM3 OE backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL2 promoter contains an ACCTAC MYB-binding motif directly recognized by MYB6. 2. MYB6 represses PAL2 promoter activity and transcript accumulation. 3. PAL2 expression is decreased in lrm3-related backgrounds and elevated by LRM3 overexpression. 4. PAL2 functions with PAL1 downstream of the LRM3-MYB6 module to promote lignin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-target with PAL1 for strengthening phenylpropanoid flux toward lignin in stem-improvement strategies.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; soybean hairy-root assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PAL2 native promoter; 35S promoter used for MYB6 repression assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem, especially lignified stem tissues/vascular tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No standalone mutant phenotype for PAL2 was reported in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stem mechanical strength, lodging resistance, and yield stability
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ubiquitin-mediated degradation of a transcriptional repressor through the 26S proteasome
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LRM3 is a U-box E3 ubiquitin ligase required for normal stem strength and lignin accumulation. 2. LRM3 directly interacts with MYB6 and promotes its ubiquitination and degradation. 3. Through MYB6 destabilization, LRM3 indirectly derepresses PAL1 and PAL2, increasing lignin biosynthesis. 4. Favorable LRM3 Hap1 was selected during domestication and is associated with stronger lignification.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for soybean lodging-resistance breeding through favorable allele selection, transgenic overexpression, or editing to enhance E3 activity; may be stacked with PAL pathway activation or MYB6 suppression.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem, especially secondary cell wall/vascular lignified tissues
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly activates PAL1 and PAL2 by promoting degradation of MYB6 repressor
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces stem strength, pod number, and grain weight per plant; no negative tradeoff from overexpression reported in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tissues, predominantly in leaves; increases in fourth internode from V3 to V7
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The C-terminal ARM repeat region (aa 306-841) is essential for MYB6 interaction. Hap1/Hap2/Hap3 differ by three SNPs; Cys360 variant shows stronger ubiquitination-associated function than Arg360.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts as an E3 ubiquitination component interacting directly with MYB6; no stable multimeric complex beyond this interaction was established
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant cloning; complementation; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; Y2H; split-LUC; Co-IP; pull-down; in vitro ubiquitination; cell-free degradation; MG132 inhibition; lignin profiling; haplotype association
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive positive upstream regulator of lignin accumulation by removing the MYB6 transcriptional repressor; central switch connecting ubiquitin proteolysis to phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: CRA024512; bulk segregant resequencing; population haplotype analysis using 1591 soybean accessions from NCBI SRA
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental stem lignification stage; domestication-selected allelic variation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation, stem mechanical strength, lodging resistance, and yield-related traits.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant U-box E3 ubiquitin ligase with ARM repeats
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MG132-sensitive proteasome pathway; field and greenhouse growth; stem developmental stages V3-V7
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tissues, predominantly in leaves; increases in lignifying stems
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis via post-translational control of a transcriptional repressor
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used in overexpression and transient assays

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stem mechanical strength, lodging resistance, and yield stability
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB6 is an R2R3-MYB transcription factor that directly binds PAL1 and PAL2 promoters. 2. MYB6 represses PAL1/PAL2 expression and promoter activity, reducing lignin biosynthesis. 3. MYB6 protein is destabilized by LRM3 via ubiquitination and the 26S proteasome. 4. Overexpression causes lodging and reduced lignification, while knockout increases lignin in hairy roots and suppresses the lrm3 defect.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Negative regulator suitable for targeted knockout or repression to improve lignin accumulation and lodging resistance, especially combined with favorable LRM3 alleles.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem, especially secondary cell wall/vascular lignified tissues
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: MYB6 overexpression causes lodging and reduced stem lignification; broader developmental effects not fully defined.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tissues with highest expression in leaves; decreases in fourth internode from V3 to V7
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Soybean homologue of poplar LTF1. MYB6 accumulation is increased in lrm3 mutants without corresponding transcript increase, consistent with post-translational control.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Directly interacts with LRM3, which mediates its ubiquitination and degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; hairy-root CRISPR knockout; Y2H; split-LUC; Co-IP; pull-down; protein abundance assay; in vitro ubiquitination; cell-free degradation; MG132 assay; EMSA; CUT&Tag-qPCR; promoter-LUC assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key transcriptional repressor that directly limits lignin biosynthesis by suppressing the committed-step PAL genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces lignin accumulation and stem strength by repressing PAL1 and PAL2.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MG132-sensitive proteasome pathway; transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; soybean hairy-root assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tissues, highest in leaves; decreases during stem lignification from V3 to V7
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis at the transcriptional level
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for overexpression and transient assays
